seoTitle: Landsdale vs Lange — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Landsdale and Lange. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Landsdale and Lange.
heading: Landsdale vs Lange — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Landsdale, Western Australia and Lange, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Landsdale has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Lange (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Landsdale or Lange safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Landsdale has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Lange scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Landsdale scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Lange scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Landsdale or Lange?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Landsdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
